text:
Hey Robert.  first comment on your videos, I think.  Do you have any idea what would happen if I chose to dope my 3d printer Resin with Graphene platelets?

also, do you have any solutions for sorting by platelet size?  Do the different size flakes react deferentially to any known forces like magnetism or perhaps centrifugal force?  If they react to the Sonic waves in the sonicator, can the frequency be tuned to specific platelet size?

author: @skaltura
text:
I've been making multiple Kg via electrochemical exfoliation using sulphates myself :)
Fun to see it's becoming easier and easier to do this, table salt? :O

Good idea on cycling sides, that would make so much more efficient use of a large bath where to do. I was thinking on my setup to start cycling voltage, but i've already made more than i need for my purposes right now. Doing some fiberglass composites.

author: @milcotto4153
text: This is not the way to do things on YouTube...at all, imo. Why isn't there a list of these things in the description so that people can buy them or borrow them where they want to? And you speak way to fast and swallow half of the words, so I don't even understand what you are saying. And it is not even possible to see the things you are talking about because of the camera is too far away. And there is not a second of pause between your sentences so that we can let anything sink in. And why would you even think that people would want to buy a screwdriver and table salt from you? Do you want us to learn this or not??

replies.author: @bell1095
replies.text: It‘s the echo within your  room, which impacts the clear voice ....

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that's exactly what it is mate

replies.author: @m3sca1
replies.text: I just had an idea that may help with the echo...rather than sound deaden the room (which is costly) stuff a microphone at the bottom of a tube with sound deadening around the tube. A pillow and cardboard roll from the kitchen foil ought be enough to test if it helps. So the sound wave can only reach the mic down the tube, and the reflections would be absorbed by the pillow. Thus eliminating the bulk of the double signal overlapping the source. Would be a very directional mic.

author: @Karabetter
text:
Dear Robert, wish you & your all important crew peace & happiness during Christmas and an extremely successful 2019!!!
Q: Maybe I am missing something, but isn't native grafoil sheet a relatively good  conductor? Wouldn't grafoil, with graphene flakes on its surface (created by your battery reversing-cycle process then simply washed to remove the NaCl) , make wonderful charge collection plates in a supercapacitor?
I realize it might be a delicate physical bond, which might not stand up well to a battery type electro-chemical process.  But capacitor charge collection would normally not be that stressful.

author: @jonlaws4493
text: Looks interesting. Could you possibly invest in some microphones as I sometimes have problems hearing the audio due to background noise.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: we probably do it in the factory mate as the cieling is lower

replies.author: @jonlaws4493
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith a radio mic or similar would help enormously as the camera one picks up all the room noise and echos. I’m getting old(er) and my ears are not what they used to be :(

replies.author: @RWBHere
replies.text:
Maybe your speakers are too good? I've only ever listened to these videos with an iPad which has a fiddly little internal speaker. The speaker loses most of the Bass, and Robert sounds, not perfect, but acceptable, through it. His audio is certainly better than it was in the early videos! Robert's voice is very Bassy. Maybe try cutting the Bass and optimising for voice in your computer's audio playback equalisation?

That said, perfectly adequate low power FM radio mics which playback either on the 49 MHz 'baby alarm' channels, on 446 MHz, or else on the 88-108 MHz FM broadcast band (simply tune to an empty frequency; most work on 88.7MHz, if memory serves me correctly, but you can tune to one side or other), only cost a few pounds. I used a couple of them for years, on different frequencies, as a wireless home intercom, with no trouble.

replies.author: @jonlaws4493
replies.text: RWBHere good point, I mostly list through headphones which do tend to give full range so lots of background comes through when I up the volume to account for the quieter voice audio due to the mic distance. It’s viewable, but could be a lot more so with better audio.

author: @bratroat451
text:
Hello Robert. I am from Russia and therefore badly write in English (via translator), sorry. Don't you think that it is better not to break the graphite in nacl and destroy in Na2CO3 ( soda ash ). Then there will be no need to neutralize Cl (chlorine) with vitamin B2 ?
 .... Merry Christmas

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: the b2 doesn't act to neutralise chlorine mate - it is a stabaliser to keep the graphene in suspension - have a good xmas too

replies.author: @aaknuu
replies.text: Can't you use zinc sulfate or an other sulfate for the same process.

replies.author: @bratroat451
replies.text: in fact, to break the compressed graphite, you can use any electrolyte. Someone uses sulfuric acid electrolyte, Robert Murray-Smith wants to use NaCl. But then Cl will stand out, it is not safe. For safety as an electrolyte, I suggest using soda ash (Na2co3). soda ash breaks more compressed graphite compared to nacl and this soda has no cl, it is safe. Sorry for bad English

author: @BushImports
text: I was really more interested in knowing exactly how to process the hemp bast to make the hemp batteries/supercaps. They say cook it at 24 hours at 350 degrees F then blast it, I assume they mean with intense heat, but that really does not make much sense because it looks to me like all that could produce is ashes. Ashes would be worthless would they not? I haven't bought any of the bast yet, I thought I would wait until I was sure I could do what I wanted before I ordered any.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I understand that mate - but other folks are interested in other things - we had a look at the comments and this seemed to be what most folks wanted. as for the bast that's exactly what they mean but do it in an argo  atmoshpere and no ash

author: @joabel19711
text:
Electrolyzing water & graphite with a repeating pulse  - leads to liquid BINGO fuel (Aqua Fuel) - which is not a gas (like oxy-hydrogen.)   
You can find a way to do it at JLNLabs.org.  
Electro-Bonding graphite to hydrogen & oxygen apparently makes a liquid.

text: You could also use a ultrasonic fogger aka mist maker. They are cheap on ebay. I have used one for a lot of graphene production, and it works sometimes faster than a ultrasonicating bath. Thanks for sharing. Looking forward to the live stream. Have a good day guys.

author: @BenjaminGoose
text:
Next video: how to make a sonicator.

Awesome Rob, can't wait!

replies.author: @BasementBen
replies.text: I've always wondered about "fusing" a 100-200watt speaker voicecoil in maybe silicone (Cone completely removed) and epoxy'ing that to a Metal Bowl and driving with a squarewave, No idea if it would work.  Gotta Love these Videos'!

replies.author: @Web3Dre
replies.text: Maybe, but probably not. At least not efficiently. Best approach would be to use an ultrasonic transducer. I wrote how to make a setup on a comment here. If you like something that works without buying an industrial ultrasonic cleaner. That would be the best approach.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: awesome mate

replies.author: @baraBober
replies.text: $50-$80 on amazon

replies.author: @Web3Dre
replies.text: @Sasha Z Keep in mind that most of the ultrasonic cleaners that's on amazon for 50$ to 80$ has some limitations. One of them are that they can only run for 30 min at a time with a recommended cool-down for about 10 to 15 minutes . So considering that you will be running one batch for 6 hours, would make it very time consuming. Also you would preferably have one that is powerful enough to do the job properly. So keep these factors in mind if you intend to buy one.
